jobs.title: Commercial Growth Account Executive (Italian Speaker)
jobs.description: <p>We are searching for an ambitious Commercial Growth Account Executive. Your role will primarily be identifying new workloads to expand MongoDB's usage into accounts that are already leveraging our Database as a Service (DBaaS), Atlas, in a self-serve manner. You'll focus on helping customers understand the continuous evolution of our developer data platform and its additional features that could be leveraged for their specific use cases. You will support customers at any stage of their journey, fostering greater adoption, retention, and overall satisfaction. This role provides an excellent opportunity for growth in a highly dynamic sales team.</p> <p>We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.</p> <h3>Day to Day</h3> <ul> <li>Identify new workloads and potential use cases within existing accounts to drive increased Atlas consumption via your own pipeline generation and outbound activities</li> <li>Articulate the value of MongoDB's expanded feature set and evolving developer data platform</li> <li>Support customers throughout their journey, addressing any queries and ensuring optimal product usage</li> <li>Develop relationships within customer accounts to promote greater visibility and deepen the partnership between MongoDB and your account's decision-makers</li> <li>Strategically navigate diverse industries with a data-driven discovery and sales process to help land and expand customer workloads</li> </ul> <h3>What you bring to the table</h3> <ul> <li>At least 1 years of Sales experience, with a preference for database or software</li> <li>Consistent record of meeting or exceeding targets in a highly competitive environment (top 10% performer)</li> <li>Dynamic, enthusiastic, tenacious, team-oriented mindset</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> <li>Experience working within a quota and commission structure</li> <li>College degree or equivalent work experience</li> <li>High EQ, coachability, intellectual curiosity, and motivation to build a career at MongoDB</li> <li>Native Italian speaker</li> </ul> <h3>Things we love</h3> <ul> <li>Familiarity with database, developer personas, and open-source technology</li> <li>Experience with sales tech stack tools like Salesforce.com, Outreach, and Chorus.ai</li> </ul> <h3>Why You Should Apply</h3> <ul> <li>We offer an industry-leading onboarding process for Associate Commercial Growth AE’s that prioritizes professional development</li> <li>High earning potential and a welcoming, inclusive workplace<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w8fOfvvUcko&amp;list=PL4RCxklHWZ9s9TQD1hPFWdOOwZuECDQb1&amp;index=39"> Meet MongoDB’s Employee Affinity Groups!</a></li> <li>Continuous career development, sales training in MEDDPIC, and Command of the Message</li> <li>Benefits include:</li> <ul> <li>Sales Bootcamp</li> <li>Cross-departmental mentor and buddy programs</li> </ul> <li>Join us at MongoDB, where your work matters, your individuality is embraced, and you have the freedom to create. You'll be a part of a culture that values your wellbeing and supports your professional and personal journey. We’re committed to making a real-world impact, and we can't wait to see what kind of difference you can make. Learn more about working at MongoDB and help us transform the world!</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p>Req ID: 426246</p>

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Acquisition
jobs.description: <h3>About The Sales Team</h3> <p>MongoDB is always developing and innovating — not only in our technology, but also in our sales go-to-market strategy. Our sales leadership is committed to building the best salesforce in technology. This means, inspiring and enabling success for everyone on the team. We not only equip you to be successful and close deals, but we want your feedback and input on how we can continue to “<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Think Big and Go Far</a>.” As a crucial part of the Sales team at MongoDB, you will have access to a lucrative market and learn how to sell from some of the most successful sales leaders in the software industry.</p> <h3>The Role</h3> <p>As an Enterprise Account Executive, Acquisition you will play a pivotal role in driving the growth and success of MongoDB. You will be responsible for identifying and pursuing new business opportunities, cultivating relationships with key stakeholders, and ensuring the successful adoption of our solutions.</p> <p>We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Mexico City for our hybrid working model.</p> <h3>What You’ll Be Doing</h3> <p>Prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT leaders, and technical end usersIdentify net-new customers through outbound prospecting&nbsp;Manage the full sales cycle for all MongoDB Products &amp; ServicesDevelop and execute a strategic territory plan to achieve revenue targetsBuild a sales pipeline in Salesforce and Clari while maximizing prospecting tools such as Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, Sendoso, and OutreachCollaborate with the sales ecosystem to drive sales initiatives and partnerships</p> <h3>What You’ll Bring To The Role</h3> <p>3+ years of full-cycle sales experience, with an emphasis on closing new customersDemonstrated success with cold calling and creative pipeline generation strategiesA proven track record of overachievement and hitting sales targetsAbility to articulate the business value of complex enterprise technologyFluent in English&nbsp;</p> <h3>Things We Love</h3> <p>Passionate about growing your career in the largest market in software (database)Previous Sales Methodology training (e.g. MEDDIC, SPIN, Challenger Sales)Familiarity with databases, develops and open source technology a plus</p> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <div>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</div> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.<br><br>Req ID: 424444</p>

jobs.title: Global Operations Associate
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><p><strong>À propos de Flexport :</strong></p> <p>Chez Flexport, nous pensons que le commerce mondial peut faire progresser l'humanité. C'est pourquoi notre mission est de rendre le commerce mondial si simple qu'il se développera. Nous façonnons l'avenir d'un secteur de 10 000 milliards de dollars grâce à des solutions basées sur une technologie innovante et des équipes exceptionnelles. Aujourd'hui, des entreprises de toutes tailles, des marques émergentes aux entreprises du Fortune 500, utilisent la technologie Flexport pour transporter chaque année plus de 19 milliards de dollars de marchandises dans 112 pays.&nbsp;</p> <p>Les récents bouleversements de la chaîne d’approvisionnement mondiale ont mis en évidence l’importance de Flexport dans le mouvement des marchandises à l’échelle internationale. Nous sommes fiers de bénéficier du soutien des meilleurs investisseurs du secteur, qui croient en notre mission, nos solutions et nos collaborateurs. Prêt(e) à relever des défis mondiaux qui ont un impact sur les entreprises, la société et l'environnement ? Rejoignez-nous !</p> <p><strong>Lieu :</strong></p> <ul> <li>Télétravail et/ou éventuelle relocalisation à court terme à Amsterdam (en fonction des besoins de l'entreprise) pendant les 4-6 premiers mois, suivie d'une relocalisation permanente à Paris.</li> </ul> <p><strong>L'opportunité :</strong></p> <p>Nous recherchons un(e) Operations Associate hautement compétent(e) pour rejoindre l'équipe des opérations européennes et soutenir l'expansion de Flexport en France. Vous passerez les 4 à 6 premiers mois intégré(e) à notre siège EMEA à Amsterdam afin de maîtriser les processus opérationnels, les systèmes et les logiciels de Flexport, tout en contribuant à notre croissance en France grâce à une qualité opérationnelle irréprochable et un service client de premier ordre.&nbsp;</p> <p>Le rôle de l'équipe des opérations est essentiel pour concrétiser la mission de Flexport : faciliter le commerce mondial pour tous. Au cœur de la satisfaction de nos clients et de la gestion de nos partenaires, elle travaille en étroite collaboration avec nos équipes technologiques afin de stimuler l'innovation à travers l'automatisation des processus. Dans le cadre de notre ambition de développer un réseau mondial, nos équipes opérationnelles sont stratégiquement réparties à travers le monde pour gérer et exécuter les services et produits logistiques que nous offrons à nos clients et partenaires. <br><br></p> <p>Flexport Global Operations place la barre très haut en s’appuyant sur les données pour piloter les chaînes d'approvisionnement de nos clients et identifier des opportunités d'amélioration continue en matière de planification, de réactivité et d’exécution globale. <br><br></p> <p>En tant que Operations Associate, vous serez responsable de la mise en place et de l'exécution du mouvement de bout en bout des expéditions au sein d'un réseau régional. Au quotidien, vous coordonnerez les opérations multimodales dans votre région. Ce rôle vise à offrir une expérience client de première classe en garantissant la qualité et la ponctualité des performances. Ce poste est idéal pour une personne dotée d'un fort esprit de résolution de problèmes, d'une véritable obsession pour la satisfaction client, d’une solide expertise opérationnelle du marché du fret français et prête à relever des défis. C'est une opportunité passionnante de contribuer à la croissance de l'entreprise et de réaliser notre mission : faciliter le commerce mondial pour tous.</p> <h2><strong>Vous allez :</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Être en « première ligne » pour garantir le plus haut niveau de qualité Flexport : les expéditions se déplacent à temps et les exceptions sont traitées de manière appropriée.&nbsp;</li> <li>De plus, soutenir votre équipe dans les demandes d’escalades de moyenne à haute importance, les mouvements de haute complexité, la communication délicate et d’autres points de contact tactiques.&nbsp;</li> <li>Vous aiderez également le responsable dans la supervision de l'équipe afin d’améliorer et de fournir d'excellentes performances, notamment en termes de qualité des données et la ponctualité des jalons d'expédition ; tout en veillant au respect des KPI et SLA internes.</li> <li>Vous suivrez les procédures opérationnelles standard (« SOP ») et participerez à la création d'une infrastructure et de procédures opérationnelles évolutives pour garantir efficacité et productivité maximales pour vos clients.&nbsp;</li> <li>Vous rechercherez en permanence des opportunités d’amélioration et mettez en œuvre les actions nécessaires.&nbsp;</li> <li>Vous représenterez personnellement la proposition de valeur de Flexport en dirigeant la mise en œuvre commerciale et l'exécution des expéditions pour les nouveaux clients et opportunités.&nbsp;</li> <li>Vous agirez en tant que point de contact principal pour les escalades internes et externes, en vous assurant de résoudre rapidement les problèmes des clients et de garantir des opérations fluides à l'origine et à la destination, en remontant les problèmes si nécessaire.&nbsp;</li> <li>Vous agirez toujours dans le meilleur intérêt de l'entreprise en évitant les pertes financières (« leakages ») et en adoptant un comportement soucieux des coûts.</li> </ul> <p>Globalement, votre équipe va :</p> <ul> <li>Coordonner de manière transparente les opérations logistiques quotidiennes avec les partenaires internes et externes tels que les compagnies maritimes, les fournisseurs de transport intermodal et local, les douanes, la gestion des voies commerciales (« trade lane »), l'entreposage, le transbordement et le transport routier.&nbsp;</li> <li>Exécuter le mouvement de bout en bout des expéditions de fret au sein d'un réseau régional (importations et exportations), y compris les mouvements intermodaux.&nbsp;</li> <li>Soutenir les expéditions gérées par les partenaires tout en favorisant leur autonomie (selon la région concernée).</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Vous avez :&nbsp;</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Un BTS Gestion des transports et logistique associée (GTLA) obtenu avec succès ou un diplôme BA/BS en logistique, chaîne d'approvisionnement, commerce ou équivalent&nbsp;</li> <li>1-2 ans d'expérience en tant qu’agent de transit international, la logistique / chaîne d'approvisionnement, et/ou les opérations de fret maritime (une expérience des opérations multimodales est un plus) en France.&nbsp;</li> <li>Connaissance et compréhension des systèmes logiciels portuaires / terminaux locaux (par exemple, Ci5 de SOGET, AP+ de MGI, CargoWise, etc.).&nbsp;</li> <li>Maîtrise de l'anglais et du français (d'autres langues européennes sont un plus)&nbsp;</li> <li>Capable de s'adapter rapidement aux nouvelles technologies et de s'épanouir dans un environnement dynamique,</li> <li>Gestion efficace du temps. Être capable de prioriser et de gérer correctement un grand volume de tâches et d'engagements&nbsp;</li> <li>Une approche pratique, axée sur les processus, une pensée structurée avec de solides capacités de résolution de problèmes&nbsp;</li> <li>Excellentes compétences en communication, relations interpersonnelles et organisationnelles&nbsp;</li> <li>Capacité à mettre en place efficacement des boucles de rétroaction au sein de l'équipe, pour un produit ou un service&nbsp;</li> <li>Travail d'équipe et établissement de relations&nbsp;</li> <li>Un état d'esprit axé sur le service client ; vous êtes obsédé(e) par la satisfaction du client et vous vous efforcez d'enquêter et de résoudre le problème du client&nbsp;</li> <li>Conception et optimisation continue des processus&nbsp;</li> <li>Résolution de problèmes : savoir établir un consensus parmi des interlocuteurs aux opinions divergentes&nbsp;</li> <li>Adopter une approche « conformité d'abord » pour satisfaire nos régulateurs et les convaincre de la valeur de Flexport, car nous opérons dans un secteur fortement réglementé.&nbsp;</li> <li>Disposé(e) à voyager régulièrement et/ou à une éventuelle relocalisation à court terme à Amsterdam (en fonction des besoins de l'entreprise) pendant les 4 à 6 premiers mois, suivie d'une relocalisation permanente à Paris.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-onsite</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.</p> <h4>Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants</h4> <p>Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants.&nbsp;By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flexport.com/privacy" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://www.flexport.com/privacy&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1613840050053000&amp;usg=AFQjCNHD63svlkD-hWHlGuGOM_bSYVrnpw">www.flexport.com/privacy</a>&nbsp;for additional information.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div>

jobs.title: Global Operations Specialist
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2><strong>Help our customers improve their global supply chains</strong></h2> <h2>The Opportunity:</h2> <p>The Global Operations Specialist is a critical part of Flexport's mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. In this role, your direct customers are the partners within your region: our Flexport Squad Operations teams and customs teams, subcontractors, carriers, and terminals. Your work directly supports Flexport’s end customers in achieving their logistics goals by executing the end-to-end movement of shipments. Most importantly, you will play a pivotal role in building the next operational model at Flexport: allowing us to effectively scale to achieve our mission.</p> <p>As a Global Operations Specialist, you are obsessed with executing a shipment lifecycle through ensuring data integrity is maintained at the highest standard and delays are minimized with urgency and bias to action.</p> <p>As a new team member in our Americas Operations group, you'll begin by immersing yourself in our company culture and mastering world-class operations. As Flexport continues to grow, there could be opportunities for future Flexporters to become founding team members in new markets, relocate internationally, and help us expand our presence around the world. If you're interested in a fast-paced career with the potential for international experience, this opportunity could offer you unmatched growth and adventure.</p> <h2><strong>You will</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Be responsible for executing the movement of containers and air freight to availability at the port or warehouse of destination&nbsp;</li> <li>Be a part of the frontline groups ensuring the highest standard of Flexport quality: shipments move on time and exceptions are handled appropriately</li> <li>Serve as the first point of escalation for issue resolution, ensuring prompt and effective resolution to provide the best customer experience.&nbsp;</li> <li>Use metrics to spot opportunities to improve and deliver great performance in your role, including data quality and shipment milestone timeliness.</li> <li>Consistently follow Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to ensure that your work satisfies internal Key Performance Indicators (<em>KPIs</em>) and Service-level Agreements (<em>SLAs</em>) in-line with productivity goals.</li> <li>Analyze data to root-cause defects and support continuous improvement efforts.&nbsp;</li> <li>Execute on shipment delivery appt coordination and any reporting functions needed to support our clients business needs</li> </ul> <h2><strong>You should have</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor's/Associate's degree OR 1+ year of relevant experience preferred. New graduates are encouraged to apply!</li> <li>Excellent customer service skills with the ability to effectively empathize and prioritize the needs of a diverse customer base. You have the ability to troubleshoot and handle challenging customer issues and can successfully de-escalate or escalate issues until they are fully resolved&nbsp;</li> <li>Fluency in English, both written and verbal</li> <li>Strong attention to detail in a high-energy, fast-paced environment</li> <li>The ability to work flexible hours: this role requires working variable hours to match when customers need us most. You may be required to work national holidays, weekends, swing or night shifts, and may be asked to render overtime based on business needs. You will be based out of Flexport’s first operations center wholly dedicated to providing the best experience for Flexport’s customers.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.</p> <h4>Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants</h4> <p>Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants.&nbsp;By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flexport.com/privacy" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://www.flexport.com/privacy&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1613840050053000&amp;usg=AFQjCNHD63svlkD-hWHlGuGOM_bSYVrnpw">www.flexport.com/privacy</a>&nbsp;for additional information.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div>

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive - UKI
jobs.description: <p>Our Enterprise Sales Executives target and close new business with Datadog’s largest, most strategic customers and prospects. In this role you’ll be focused on uncovering the pain points organizations face as they operate in or migrate to a cloud environment at scale as well as delivering the appropriate Datadog solution.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prospect into large Fortune 1000 companies while running an efficient sales process</li> <li>Maintain, build and own specific relationship maps for your territory including existing relationships and aspirational contacts</li> <li>Develop a deep comprehension of customer's business</li> <li>Negotiate favorable pricing and business terms with large commercial enterprises by selling value and ROI</li> <li>Handle existing customer expectations while expanding reach and depth into assigned territory</li> <li>Demonstrate resourcefulness when faced with challenges that defy easy solution</li> <li>Have intuitive sense of necessary steps to close business and gain customer validation</li> <li>Identify robust set of business drivers behind all opportunities</li> <li>Ensure high forecasting accuracy and consistency</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Someone with 3+ years closing experience (mix of field selling within mid-market or enterprise)</li> <li>Driven and have met/exceeded direct sales goals of 1M+ and operated with an average deal size of $100k+</li> <li>Able to demonstrate methodology to prospect and build pipeline on your own</li> <li>Experienced in working for an innovative tech company (SaaS, IT infrastructure or similar preferred)</li> <li>Experienced in selling into large Fortune 1000 companies with the ability to win new logos</li> <li>Role requires regular travel to client sites, within your area and other regions, using various modes of transportation (car, train, air), depending on business needs.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous and competitive medical benefits package</li> <li>Retirement savings match</li> <li>Pet adoption and insurance program</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive (UKI) - Public Sector
jobs.description: <p>Our Enterprise Sales Executives target and close new business with Datadog’s largest, most strategic customers and prospects. In this role you’ll be focused on uncovering the pain points organizations face as they operate in or migrate to a cloud environment at scale as well as delivering the appropriate Datadog solution.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prospect into large Fortune 1000 companies while running an efficient sales process</li> <li>Maintain, build and own specific relationship maps for your territory including existing relationships and aspirational contacts</li> <li>Develop a deep comprehension of customer's business</li> <li>Negotiate favorable pricing and business terms with large commercial enterprises by selling value and ROI</li> <li>Handle existing customer expectations while expanding reach and depth into assigned territory</li> <li>Demonstrate resourcefulness when faced with challenges that defy easy solution</li> <li>Have intuitive sense of necessary steps to close business and gain customer validation</li> <li>Identify robust set of business drivers behind all opportunities</li> <li>Ensure high forecasting accuracy and consistency</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Someone with 3+ years closing experience (mix of field selling within mid-market or enterprise)</li> <li>Driven and have met/exceeded direct sales goals of 1M+ and operated with an average deal size of $100k+</li> <li>Able to demonstrate methodology to prospect and build pipeline on your own</li> <li>Experienced in working for an innovative tech company (SaaS, IT infrastructure or similar preferred)</li> <li>Experienced in selling into large Fortune 1000 companies with the ability to win new logos</li> <li>Role requires regular travel to client sites, within your area and other regions, using various modes of transportation (car, train, air), depending on business needs</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
jobs.description: <p>As a Sales Development Representative (SDR), you will prospect, qualify, and generate customer leads to assist in Datadog’s overall business growth segment. By partnering with internal stakeholders, you will help IT and Technology innovators across markets recognize Datadog’s impact in their digital transformation and migration to the cloud. SDRs have the opportunity to grow their careers in Sales and continue contributing to Datadog team success.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with various Datadog teams</li> <li>Drive initial prospect qualification and schedule discovery meetings</li> <li>Develop, present, and implement strategies for acquiring new business</li> <li>Conduct outbound outreach by cold calling and emailing prospective customers&nbsp;</li> <li>Learn to follow a well-defined methodology to help identify a customer's unique needs&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Motivated by a career in sales</li> <li>Someone with an innate curiosity to learn</li> <li>Have a desire to succeed alongside teammates</li> <li>Proven in your written and verbal communication&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Comfortable with being able to learn from rejection</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Sales Sourcer II, Business Recruiting
jobs.description: <p>We are looking for a Recruiting Sourcer to play a key role in the continued growth and success of Datadog by finding exceptional talent. The Recruiting Sourcer will partner closely with Recruiters to develop creative and tailored strategies to engage passive candidates. You will be the first point of contact for new candidates in the hiring process, playing a critical role in identifying great candidates and telling the Datadog story.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generate and maintain active and passive candidate pipeline for complex, hard-to-fill positions using various sources such as: LinkedIn Recruiter, Eightfold, Sourcing Campaigns, Google X Ray searching, previous event attendee lists, etc..&nbsp;</li> <li>Amplify and champion job openings internally and work with our employee base to find hidden talent in their networks.&nbsp;</li> <li>Develop and implement strategic sourcing plans and track progress throughout the search to be shared with stakeholders</li> <li>Use your own research to get creative on where to find talent that otherwise might stay off the radar including diverse talent</li> <li>Map out the talent market, keeping a pulse of talent flow, market attrition, and which companies employ the talent we are after&nbsp;</li> <li>Specialize in event/project management, messaging analysis, creative prospecting, and stakeholder engagement&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>An established Recruiter or Sourcer with 3+ years of experience working for a search firm or in-house recruiting team for high volume recruiting</li> <li>An individual with a high sense of urgency, strong communication skills and detail-oriented</li> <li>Tenacious and passionate about sourcing and engaging candidates&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to work independently and simultaneously on multiple roles</li> <li>An individual with a proven track record of engaging talent through tools with an ability to turn passive prospects into candidates&nbsp;</li> <li>Creative, innovative, intellectually curious and results driven</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Why You Should Apply:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive benefits package</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities&nbsp;</li> <li>Product training to develop an in-depth understanding of our product and space</li> <li>Best in breed onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and buddy program cross-departmentally</li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Sourcer II, Business Recruiting - Japan
jobs.description: <p>We are looking for a Recruiting Sourcer to play a key role in the continued growth and success of Datadog by finding exceptional talent. The Recruiting Sourcer will partner closely with Recruiters to develop creative and tailored strategies to engage passive candidates. You will be the first point of contact for new candidates in the hiring process, playing a critical role in identifying great candidates and telling the Datadog story.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generate and maintain active and passive candidate pipeline for complex, hard-to-fill positions using various sources such as: LinkedIn Recruiter, Gem, Sourcing Campaigns, Google X Ray searching, previous event attendee lists, etc..&nbsp;</li> <li>Amplify and champion job openings internally and work with our employee base to find hidden talent in their networks.&nbsp;</li> <li>Develop and implement strategic sourcing plans and track progress throughout the search to be shared with stakeholders</li> <li>Use your own research to get creative on where to find talent that otherwise might stay off the radar including diverse talent</li> <li>Map out the talent market, keeping a pulse of talent flow, market attrition, and which companies employ the talent we are after&nbsp;</li> <li>Specialize in event/project management, messaging analysis, creative prospecting, and stakeholder engagement&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>An established Recruiter or Sourcer with 3+ years of experience working for a search firm or in-house recruiting team for high volume recruiting</li> <li>An individual with a high sense of urgency, strong communication skills and detail-oriented</li> <li>Tenacious and passionate about sourcing and engaging candidates&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to work independently and simultaneously on multiple roles</li> <li>An individual with a proven track record of engaging talent through tools with an ability to turn passive prospects into candidates&nbsp;</li> <li>Creative, innovative, intellectually curious and results driven</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive benefits package</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities&nbsp;</li> <li>Product training to develop an in-depth understanding of our product and space</li> <li>Best in breed onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and buddy program cross-departmentally</li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. jobs.title: Bridge - Operations Associate, Financial Crimes (AML Investigations)
jobs.description: <h2>Who we are</h2> <h3>About Stripe</h3> <p><em>At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. <br><br>We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.</em><em>We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.</em></p> <h3><strong>About Bridge Operations and the Team</strong></h3> <p>As Stripe’s user base and global footprint grow dramatically, especially as <strong>crypto and stablecoins</strong> fundamentally change the way money is transferred, Stripe acquired <strong>Bridge</strong> to leverage its stablecoin infrastructure for global payments. Stablecoins facilitate faster, cheaper, and more efficient transactions than traditional methods, which creates unique compliance challenges.</p> <p>The <strong>Bridge Operations</strong> strategy provides operational leverage and expands Stripe’s portfolio of operational capabilities to support the scaled needs in this rapidly evolving space. This role will be part of the <strong>AML Team within the Financial Crimes organization</strong> in Bridge Operations that is accountable for monitoring, investigating, and reporting suspicious activity indicative of money laundering and other financial crimes.</p> <h3><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h3> <p>We are looking for an investigator who is passionate about financial crimes, the intricacies of blockchain analysis, and is comfortable operating in ambiguity to solve complex puzzles. You will be responsible for applying your crypto expertise to the core AML function for our Bridge platform.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Conduct comprehensive<strong> </strong>AML Investigations from beginning to end, specializing in activity involving cryptocurrency, stablecoins, and associated fiat on/off-ramps.</li> <li>Utilize blockchain analytics tools (e.g., Chainalysis, TRM Labs, Elliptic, etc.) to map, trace, and analyze transaction flows on various distributed ledgers.</li> <li>Conduct thorough open-source intelligence (OSINT) and public records research to support the identification of bad actors and illicit financial networks.</li> <li>Draft and file high-quality, defensible Suspicious Activity / Transaction Reports (SARs / STRs) with various Financial Intelligence Units based on the investigative findings.</li> <li>Review and disposition high-risk Transaction Monitoring alerts with a specific focus on crypto-related financial crime typologies.</li> <li>Document in detail the rationale for the investigative process and recommendations, ensuring full compliance and audit readiness.</li> <li>Maintain operational excellence across various workstreams and demonstrate sound judgment in navigating non-binary, high-risk decisions.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Who you are</strong></h3> <p>We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role.</p> <p><strong>Minimum requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of analytical or investigative experience in financial crimes, AML, risk, or fraud, with at least 2 years focused specifically on crypto/blockchain financial crime.</li> <li>Demonstrated experience using blockchain analytics tools (Chainalysis, TRM, Elliptic, etc.) or other tools to map, trace, and analyze blockchain transactions.</li> <li>Proven ability to draft and file high-quality SARs/STRs based on complex financial and crypto investigations.</li> <li>Strong research, writing, and analytical skills, with a focus on OSINT methodologies.</li> <li>Ability to demonstrate a strong understanding of financial crime typologies and patterns utilized to exploit financial institutions, particularly in the crypto ecosystem.</li> <li>Ability to synthesize large amounts of data (including on-chain and off-chain transaction data) and identify key trends.</li> <li>Strong English written and verbal capabilities.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>ACAMS or similar certification.</li> <li>Previous experience working in a Compliance function within a FinTech, Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP), or similar regulated entity.</li> <li>Knowledge of global stablecoin regulatory frameworks.</li> <li>Proficiency in English</li> </ul>

jobs.title: Collections Specialist
jobs.description: <h2><strong>Who we are </strong></h2> <h3><strong>About Stripe</strong></h3> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.</span></p> <p>We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.</p> <h3><strong>About the team</strong></h3> <p>Stripe was built with simplicity in mind. We strive to deliver frictionless experiences for all of our users, whether they are an Independent Business, Startup, SMB, or Enterprise and our mission is to provide all Stripe Users with the best support experience possible. Today, Stripe handles over a million support cases per year and processes millions of internal transactions.&nbsp; We’re going to achieve excellence by thinking of support in a novel, solution-oriented way, and viewing operations as an integral enabler of all of Stripe’s growth.</p> <p>Stripe has unique operational problems resulting from both our type of scale and the type of businesses we partner with as a result of “growing the GDP of the Internet.”&nbsp; Stripes leverage understanding of our products, the financial industry and money movement, and our processes to support both internal and external users. Doing so requires us to invest significant resources into building this experience ourselves.</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <p>As a Collections Specialist for Stripe Capital, you will provide surprisingly great, empathetic, and strategic support to Stripe Capital merchants who have missed payments. You will manage a collections queue of overdue users, reaching out by phone, email and SMS to assist them getting their business back on track.&nbsp; With high EQ and a genuine desire to help their businesses succeed, you’ll ensure a positive experience on Stripe while also protecting Stripe’s ability to offer more loans to more merchants.</p> <p>Perhaps most importantly, you’ll be expected to make process and product recommendations to improve users’ Collections experience and enable Stripe to offer more loans to more businesses.&nbsp; As an expert resource for the Capital product and engineering team, as well as risk, support and other functions across Stripe, you will work with these stakeholders to advocate for and in some cases drive those product and process enhancements.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Manage multiple delinquent accounts for debt collection efforts</li> <li>Keep track of assigned accounts to identify outstanding debts</li> <li>Plan a course of action to recover outstanding payments</li> <li>Negotiate payoff deadlines or payment plans</li> <li>Handle merchant questions or complaints</li> <li>Investigate and resolve discrepancies in payments or accounts</li> <li>Propose process, tooling, and product enhancements to improve the Collections experience for Stripe merchants</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</span></p> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Office and database software skills</li> <li>Ability to pick up new tooling and technology quickly&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to explain complex financial topics succinctly and clearly (eg, the collection experience to other teams within Stripe)</li> <li>Multi-tasking, time management, and prioritization skills</li> <li>Ability to grasp and navigate relevant legal requirements&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to work independently, with strong attention to detail</li> <li>Negotiation and conflict resolution skills</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication skills</li> </ul> <h3>&nbsp;</h3>

This team will directly impact the company’s bottom line and growth capabilities by supporting new and emerging businesses. The Credit Operations team is responsible for conducting credit risk assessments, underwriting small and mid-size users, and proposing mitigation strategies that enable Stripe to take smart risks for our users.</span></p> <h3>Responsibilities</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Work with merchants, from successful venture-backed startups to well established institutions; evaluating their business models and financial statements</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Help manage Stripe’s portfolio by developing a deep understanding of risk across industries, geographies, and operating models</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Identify differing risks of complex business models and enforce appropriate loss mitigation actions based on your assessment</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Utilize analytics to optimize strategies which can be enforced across Stripe’s portfolio</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Identify risk factors and trends and further report these identifiers to the wider risk team to ensure consistency in partnership</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Identify gaps in current systems, policies and strategies, and recommend enhancements and process improvements</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Have an opportunity to learn how Stripe operates from ground up and make a real impact on the future on organization&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Be a part of building a brand new team and operational culture for Stripe&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Partner with global operations specialists or business process owners to run a critical workflow</span></li> </ul> <h2>Who you are</h2> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</span></p> <h3>Minimum requirements</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">A minimum of 1+ years of experience with knowledge of commercial credit underwriting, financial risk modeling and cash flow forecasting&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Ability to transform business and financial data into a cohesive narrative that outlines potential credit risks</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Strong knowledge of accounting principles and comfort with evaluating financial statements and business models across a range of industries</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You excel in analytical thinking and problem solving</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You have a process-oriented mindset and ability to get things done</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You enjoy working in an in-office environment with strong cross team collaboration and support</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You are able to prioritize and enjoy working in a quick-moving environment</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You are humble and have a proven track record for working well across teams and with external partners</span></li> <li>You're willing to periodically work a weekend day for which you will receive a weekday off; the SDC operates during daytime hours with shifts from the morning through early evening</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Preferred qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Proficiency in SQL or other data analysis tools</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">The ability and desire to challenge the status quo</span></li> </ul>

jobs.title: Fraud Operations Associate SDC
jobs.description: <h2>Who we are</h2> <h3>About Stripe</h3> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.</p> <h2>What you’ll do</h2> <h3>Responsibilities</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Investigate accounts to identify fraudulent merchants, card testing, account takeover, and other fraud vectors</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Conduct investigations on merchant accounts to support financial partner requirements</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Assess risk factors and trends of potentially fraudulent activity</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Help maintain fraud detection strategies and business rules that balance fraud loss and customer experience</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Contribute to root cause analysis to identify opportunities to predict, prevent, and mitigate risk within process and performance</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Participate in monthly, quarterly, and annual business reviews for the Fraud Operations team</span></li> </ul> <h2>Who you are</h2> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</span></p> <h3>Minimum requirements</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Some experience in fraud analysis, fraud investigations, or fraud operations</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Experience using data to make assessments/judgements</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">A team-focused mentality and effective problem solving skills</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">The ability to approach challenges from a user perspective while being pragmatic &amp; solutions oriented</span></li> <li>You have availability to work 100% in office</li> <li style="font-weight: 400;">You're willing to periodically work a weekend (remotely) for which you will be compensated with week days off</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Preferred qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Fraud experience in payments, e-commerce, or fintech mitigating digital/card-not-present fraud</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Knowledge of fraud schemes, fraud prevention methods, and fraud detection tools</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Prior fraud experience across multiple industries/verticals</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Experience in a high growth technology company</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Experience working from a queue</span></li> </ul>

jobs.title: Operations Associate, Financial Crimes (AML Investigations)
jobs.description: <h2>Who we are</h2> <h3>About Stripe</h3> <p><em>At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. <br><br>We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.</em><em>We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.</em></p> <h3>About the team</h3> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">This role will be part of the Anti Money Laundering (AML) Team within the Financial Crimes organization in SDC that is accountable for monitoring, investigating and reporting suspicious activity indicative of money laundering and other financial crimes.</span></p> <h2>What you’ll do</h2> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We are looking for someone who is passionate about financial crimes and solving the puzzle of investigations, is naturally curious, and is comfortable operating in ambiguity and finding creative solutions. You will be responsible for reviewing and dispositioning Transaction Monitoring alerts, conducting AML Investigations, and drafting and filing&nbsp; Suspicious Activity / Transaction Report (SARs / STRs) with various Financial Intelligence U</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">nits.</span></p> <h3>Responsibilities</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Conducting AML Investigations from beginning to end, including conducting transaction analysis and open source and public records research in various jurisdictions;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Documenting in detail the rationale for the investigative process and recommendations of each review;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Drafting and filing Suspicious Activity / Transaction Report (SARs / STRs) with various Financial Intelligence Units; and</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Maintaining operational excellence across various workstreams;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Reviewing and dispositioning Transaction Monitoring alerts;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Demonstrating sound judgment and an ability to navigate non-binary, high-risk decisions.</span></li> </ul> <h2>Who you are</h2> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</span></p> <h3>Minimum requirements</h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Strong English written and verbal capabilities;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">2+ years of analytical or investigative experience, preferably within the financial crimes, AML, risk, threat analysis, or fraud fields;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Strong research, writing, and analytical skills;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Ability to demonstrate an understanding of financial crime typologies and patterns utilized to exploit financial institutions;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Ability to synthesize large amounts of information such as transaction data and identify key trends;&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Ability to work efficiently and independently in a fast-paced environment; and</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Independently analyze and evaluate information from various data sources to determine a course of action for a matched case.</span></li> </ul> <h3><strong>Preferred qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Knowledge/ experience in the payments and the fintech industry;&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Previous experience working in a Compliance function;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">ACAMS or similar certification.</span></li> </ul>

Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Today, Stripe handles over a million support cases per year and processes millions of internal transactions. We’re going to achieve excellence by thinking of support in a novel, solution-oriented way, and viewing operations as an integral enabler of all of Stripe’s growth.</p> <h3><strong>About the team</strong></h3> <p>At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.</p> <p>We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have the opportunity to join an operations team across Risk, Product Support, and Financial Crimes.&nbsp;</li> <li>Troubleshoot and solve complex problems.&nbsp;</li> <li>Analyze our processes and instigate changes to help scale our operations and improve user experience.&nbsp;</li> <li>Work closely with other Stripe teams across the globe.&nbsp;</li> <li>Gain unique insights into how thousands of businesses on Stripe scale and operate.&nbsp;</li> <li>Have an opportunity to learn how Stripe operates from ground up and make a real impact on the future of the organization.&nbsp;</li> <li>Be a part of building a brand new team and operational culture for Stripe.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p>We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply.&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Minimum requirements</strong></h3> <ul> <li>You are a recent graduate with less than 12 months of full time working experience or a 2025 or 2026 graduate with full time availability.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>You have excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.&nbsp;</li> <li>You have a user first mindset and are energized by the challenge of solving difficult problems.&nbsp;</li> <li>You excel in analytical thinking and problem solving.&nbsp;</li> <li>You enjoy learning about technical products and processes, you are an organized, self-starter with passion for finding solutions to our user’s challenges.&nbsp;</li> <li>You have a process-oriented mindset and ability to get things done.&nbsp;</li> <li>You enjoy working in an in-office environment with strong cross team collaboration and support.&nbsp;</li> <li>You are able to prioritize and enjoy working in a quick-moving environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>You are humble and have a proven track record for working well across teams and with external partners.</li> </ul>
